title: Privacy Policy (GDPR)
s2Intro: Depending on how you use the Service, we may process:
s2L1Body: email, login identifiers, and profile data.
s2L2Body: visited pages, actions performed, timestamps, and technical logs required for operation and security.
s2L3Body: text, documents, and other data you submit for Service features (including AI features).
s2L4Body: messages and interactions with our team.
s2L5Body: payment-related data processed via payment providers as needed to complete transactions.
s3L1: Provide, maintain, and secure the Service.
s3L3: Process your inputs to generate AI-powered outputs.
s3L4: Provide customer support and respond to requests.
s3L5: Comply with legal obligations (for example accounting and regulatory obligations).
s3L6: Improve the Service and prevent fraud, in line with GDPR requirements.
s4Intro: We process personal data in accordance with applicable law, including Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (“GDPR”). Legal bases include:
s4L1Body: (Article 6(1)(b)): providing the Service and account-related operations.
s4L3Body: (Article 6(1)(f)): security, fraud prevention, and Service improvement, subject to your rights.
s5Intro: We may share personal data with:
s5L1: technical processors (for example hosting and infrastructure providers);
s6Body: If we rely on providers outside the EU/EEA, we implement appropriate safeguards under GDPR (for example adequacy decisions, Standard Contractual Clauses, and supplementary measures).
s7Intro: We retain personal data only for as long as needed for the purposes described:
s7L1: Account data: while your account remains active and as required to meet legal obligations and handle potential claims.
s7L2: Security/technical logs: as required for diagnostics, security, and maintenance.
s7L3: Request-related content: as needed to provide the Service and resolve disputes.
s7Outro: For more details, contact us at privacy@zedos.app.
s8P1: We may use cookies and similar technologies necessary for Service operation, security, and user experience.
s8P2: Where optional cookies require consent, consent will be collected and managed in accordance with applicable law.
s9Intro: Under GDPR, you may have the following rights (depending on your situation):
s9L7: Right to withdraw consent where processing is based on consent.
s9L8: Right to lodge a complaint with the CNIL.
s10Title: 10. AI Act: Transparency on AI Use
s10Intro: When you use AI features, your prompts and related usage information may be processed to generate outputs. We implement reasonable measures to comply with applicable transparency and compliance obligations.
s10L1: AI outputs may require human verification and can be inaccurate or incomplete.
s10L2: The Service is an assistance tool; you remain responsible for how outputs are used.
s11Body: We apply technical and organizational safeguards to protect personal data against loss, unauthorized access, alteration, and disclosure.
s12Body: We may update this policy from time to time. The update date appears at the top of this page.
